metadata.title: Veo 3.1 API
metadata.description: Test and integrate Veo 3.1 API for cinematic text-to-video, image-to-video, first-and-last-frame control, extension, and 4K-ready video workflows.

title: Veo 3.1 API
description: Veo 3.1 is Google's latest line of video generation models, built for cinematic text-to-video and image-to-video creation with stronger prompt following, native audio workflows, and more controllable shots.
Use this page to compare Lite, Fast, Quality, Extend, 1080P, and 4K-oriented variants, then move from playground testing into a production API request with predictable payload fields.
pricingNote: USD estimates use $0.005/credit with the best annual plan. Final billing follows the selected Veo 3.1 variant, resolution, and task type.

labels.apiDocsDetailDescription: Open the full API documentation for endpoints, authentication, task polling, and payload details.
labels.apiInputDescription: Keep request payloads clear across text prompts, image references, duration, aspect ratio, resolution, callbacks, and task retrieval.
labels.selectedModelNote: The playground, request sample, request fields, and pricing on this page follow the selected model type.

featuresTitle: What Veo 3.1 API adds to your video workflow
featuresDescription: The page follows the same API-first structure as the reference: test the model, understand the fields, then move into integration.

capabilities.title: Text and image to video
capabilities.description: Generate cinematic clips from prompts or starting images, with variants for fast iteration and higher-quality final output.

capabilities.title: First and last frame control
capabilities.description: Guide transitions with beginning and ending frames so motion, composition, and shot direction stay closer to the creative brief.

capabilities.title: Native audio workflows
capabilities.description: Build videos that account for sound, dialogue-like ambience, and richer audio direction where the selected Veo 3.1 mode supports it.

capabilities.title: Extend and upscale paths
capabilities.description: Use dedicated Extend, 1080P, and 4K variants when the workflow needs longer scenes, higher resolution, or post-generation retrieval.

quickStartTitle: From prompt test to Veo 3.1 API request
quickStartDescription: A compact integration path for teams that want to validate motion quality before wiring Veo 3.1 into an app or content pipeline.

workflows.title: 1. Choose a variant
workflows.description: Select Lite, Fast, Quality, Extend, 1080P, or 4K based on iteration speed, output quality, and delivery target.

workflows.title: 2. Add creative inputs
workflows.description: Provide a prompt, optional image references, duration, aspect ratio, resolution, and callback settings for the selected model.

workflows.title: 3. Poll and deliver
workflows.description: Track the task until it succeeds, then retrieve the generated video URL for your app, campaign, or production pipeline.

useCasesTitle: What you can create with Veo 3.1 API
useCasesDescription: Use the API when your product needs repeatable, high-quality AI video output rather than one-off manual exports.
useCases: Product launch videos and hero clips
Social ads for TikTok, Reels, and Shorts
Image-to-video animations for product and character assets
Extended or higher-resolution clips for production review

communityShowcase.title: Successful Veo 3.1 generations from the community
communityShowcase.description: Browse public Veo 3.1 outputs that finished successfully, then open any case to inspect the prompt, settings, and result.

productionTitle: Built for real Veo 3.1 API workflows

productionNotes.title: Account-gated execution
productionNotes.body: Users sign in, submit jobs, and consume credits through the existing SdanceAI generation flow.

productionNotes.title: Reference media handling
productionNotes.body: The studio supports uploaded reference media, direct URLs, previews, and form/API payload editing for Veo 3.1 variants.

productionNotes.title: Resolution-aware delivery
productionNotes.body: Choose a lower-cost mode for early prompt iteration, then move to Quality, 1080P, or 4K paths when the direction is ready.

faqs.question: What is Veo 3.1 API best for?
faqs.answer: It is best for cinematic text-to-video and image-to-video generation where motion quality, prompt control, frame guidance, and production-ready delivery matter.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Lite, Fast, and Quality?
faqs.answer: Lite is the lower-cost entry point, Fast is tuned for quicker iteration, and Quality focuses on higher-fidelity output when creative direction is already clear.

faqs.question: Can Veo 3.1 use first and last frames?
faqs.answer: Yes. Veo 3.1 workflows can use frame guidance to generate transitions between a starting frame and an ending frame, depending on the selected variant and payload.

faqs.question: Does Veo 3.1 support image-to-video?
faqs.answer: Yes. The workflow supports prompt-only generation and image-guided generation, so you can animate product shots, characters, scenes, or visual concepts.

faqs.question: Which output formats are common for Veo 3.1?
faqs.answer: Veo 3.1 video workflows commonly target short MP4 clips with 16:9 or 9:16 aspect ratios, with resolution options depending on the selected variant.

faqs.question: When should I use Extend?
faqs.answer: Use Extend when you already have a generated video task and want to continue the scene rather than start a new clip from scratch.

faqs.question: Where can I test it before integrating?
faqs.answer: Use the playground on this page to test prompts, references, resolution, aspect ratio, duration, and API payload behavior before moving into production.

faqs.question: Can I use Veo 3.1 API for commercial projects?
faqs.answer: This page is configured for commercial-use workflows. For production use, review your account plan, credit balance, model availability, and any applicable content policy requirements.

cta.title: Turn Veo 3.1 into your video workflow
cta.description: Open the generator, test a prompt or image reference, then use the API docs when you are ready to wire Veo 3.1 into production.
